workflow.critical: Think like a Product Manager and Tech Lead combined — bridge technical findings to business outcomes
Translate EVERY significant technical issue into its business consequence: who is affected, what is the impact, what is the risk magnitude
Run AFTER GR/SR/PR/AR so you can reference their findings and elevate them to business language

Finding severities: 🔴 BLOCKER | 🟡 WARNING | 🟢 SUGGESTION | ❓ QUESTION. Use ❓ when business intent or user impact cannot be determined from the diff alone — e.g., "Is this behavior change intentional from a product perspective?", "Were users informed of this breaking change?". A QUESTION that gets a bad answer becomes a BLOCKER or WARNING depending on the business consequence.

step.goal: Load PR context, prior findings, and prepare business analysis

check.output: ❌ No PR selected. Please run [SP] Select PR first.

step.action: Read {pr_context} to get: target_branch, base_branch, pr_type, pr_knowledge_base, completed_reviews
Load PR-specific knowledge base from {pr_knowledge_base} — extract issue_context (acceptance criteria) if available from MCP tools

Adapt review scope: use knowledge_base.stack_context, knowledge_base.files_analysis, and knowledge_base.reviewer_guidance.business_review to adjust business focus based on detected project type and domain.
Load findings already collected from completed reviews (GR, SR, PR, AR) to translate them into business impact
step.output: 💼 Starting Business Review ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ Focus: User Impact | Business Risk | Feature Completeness Data Safety | Observability | Cross-cutting Concerns PR type: {pr_type} | Prior reviews loaded: {completed_reviews} ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

step.action: Determine what this PR is supposed to deliver based on: branch name, PR description, commit messages, issue context from MCP (if available)

check-list.item: Does the implementation cover the full scope described in the branch name / PR title?
If acceptance criteria available (from Jira/Linear MCP): check each AC item against the diff — implemented or missing?
Are error states handled from the user's perspective? (not just technically caught, but communicated clearly)
Are loading states present for async operations that users will notice?
Are empty states handled? (empty list, no results, zero state)
Edge cases that affect real users: very long inputs, special characters, concurrent users, offline state
Demo / test data or hardcoded values that should not reach production

step.output-format: 🔴/🟡/🟢/❓ [COMPLETENESS] — **Description** → User impact: what happens to the user if this is shipped as-is → Suggested fix or question: what needs to be added/changed, or what to ask the author

step.action: Trace the user journey through the changed code: what flows are affected, what changes for the user

check-list.item: UX regressions: does any existing user flow break or degrade?
Behavior changes: is existing behavior changed in a way users rely on? (implicit contract broken)
New flows: are new features intuitive? Is the UI clear about what actions do?
Destructive actions: are irreversible actions (delete, clear, migrate) clearly communicated with confirmation?
Feedback loops: do users know when operations succeed or fail? Are there success/error messages?
Performance from user perspective: will users notice slowness? (N+1 queries → slow page load, missing debounce → laggy search)
Accessibility: keyboard navigation, screen reader labels (aria), color contrast, focus management
Mobile/responsive: does UI work on small screens if app is used on mobile?

step.goal: Business risk assessment — translate technical findings
step.action: For each 🔴 finding from prior reviews (SR, GR, PR, AR), assess the business consequence

check-list.item: Security vulnerabilities → business risk: - XSS: can lead to account takeover, data theft, GDPR breach, reputational damage - Hardcoded credentials: instant unauthorized access if code is public - Broken auth: unauthorized access to protected resources - Token forgeable: privilege escalation, account impersonation
Data loss risk: operations that silently lose user data (fire-and-forget DB writes, missing rollback)
Downtime risk: breaking changes that could cause failures in production (migration without fallback, schema change)
Compliance risk: GDPR (user data exposure), accessibility laws (WCAG), industry regulations
Revenue/retention risk: bugs that directly affect core user workflows → churn
Support burden: confusing UX or silent errors → high support ticket volume
Scale risk: patterns that work with test data but fail at production scale (N+1 with 10k records)

step.action: Identify any changes that affect existing user data or storage mechanisms

check-list.item: Storage migration: if switching storage (e.g. localStorage → IndexedDB), what happens to existing user data?
Schema changes: are existing records compatible with new schema? Is there a migration script?
Data seeding: does seeding run every time? Could it overwrite user-created data?
Multi-user isolation: is data properly scoped per user? Can User A access User B's data?
Rollback plan: if this deploy fails or has a bug, can we roll back without data loss?
Backward compatibility: does old code still work if new schema is deployed (blue/green, canary)?
Analytics: are new features tracked so we can measure adoption and success?
Error monitoring: are errors surfaced to a monitoring system (not just console.error)?
Logging: are key business events logged for audit/debug? (login, data mutation, auth failure)
Feature flags: is this behind a flag so it can be disabled without a deploy if issues arise?
Success metrics: how will the team know this feature is working as intended post-deploy?
Internationalization: are there hardcoded strings that would need translation in a multi-language product?
Browser compatibility: do new APIs (IndexedDB, btoa, CSS features) work across supported browsers?
Breaking API contracts: does this change any interfaces that other teams or services depend on?
Environment parity: are there dev-only artifacts that could leak to production? (demo hints, console logs, mock data)
Third-party dependencies: are new packages added? License compatible? Bundle size impact on users?
Deployment order: does this require coordinated deployment with backend or other services?

step.goal: Compile business impact and write findings
step.action: Assign overall business risk level: CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW | MINIMAL
step.risk-matrix: CRITICAL = data breach risk, auth bypass, mass data loss, compliance violation HIGH = significant UX regression, silent data loss for some users, performance cliff at scale MEDIUM = missing features from AC, confusing UX, no observability, rollback concerns LOW = minor UX gaps, no analytics, non-critical missing edge cases MINIMAL = additive feature, no regressions, low risk changes
step.action: Apply any business-specific guidance from knowledge_base.reviewer_guidance.business_review and knowledge_base.relevant_guidelines to generate additional business checks beyond the default categories above. If no project-specific business guidance exists, skip silently.

Structure the output by category, ordered by severity within each section: - Feature Completeness gaps (🔴 first, then 🟡, 🟢, ❓) - User Impact issues - Business Risk items (translated from technical findings) - Data/Migration concerns - Observability gaps - Cross-cutting issues - ❓ Questions for Author (consolidated at the end)

Write a "Business Verdict" section: - Overall risk level - Top 3 business concerns - Deployment recommendation: ship now / ship with fixes / do not ship - Post-ship monitoring: what to watch after deploy
Update {pr_context}: add 'business-review' to completed reviews list
step.output: 💼 Business review complete. Business risk: {risk_level} {blocker_count} blockers (🔴), {warning_count} warnings (🟡), {suggestion_count} suggestions (🟢), {question_count} questions (❓) for author. Run [RR] Generate Report to compile all findings.
